items.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
items.description: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

items.title: Man who hacked US Supreme Court filing system sentenced to probation
items.description: Nicholas Moore hacked into three U.S. government networks using stolen credentials, and then bragged about it and posted victims' personal data on Instagram under the handle @ihackedthegovernment.

items.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
items.description: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vulnerabilities in real-life attacks, according to a cybersecurity firm.

items.title: Gigs turns your concert history into a personal live music archive
items.description: New iPhone app Gigs uses AI to turn old tickets, screenshots, and emails into a personal concert archive with stats, memories, and more.

items.title: Chef Robotics escaped the robot cooking graveyard and says it’s thriving — here’s why
items.description: The company, which deploys AI-guided robot arms for food production, says it is looking to expand its services to provide for a broader array of customers.

items.title: Uber will now pick up your returns from your doorstep
items.description: The new returns feature is the company's latest effort to become an everything app. There are limitations to the new service, and there is, of course, a courier fee.

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals
items.description: The company says Claude Design is intended to help people like founders and product managers without a design background share their ideas more easily.

items.title: With US spy laws set to expire, lawmakers are split over protecting Americans from warrantless surveillance

items.title: Google’s AI Mode can now help you find products in stock nearby
items.description: Although you can already track hotel prices at the city level, the new update lets you do so for a specific hotel that you're interested in.

items.title: Bluesky confirms DDoS attack is cause of continued app outages
items.description: Bluesky has been experiencing ongoing service disruptions since just before 3 a.m. ET on April 15.

items.title: Netflix plans to add a vertical video feed, use AI for recommendations
items.description: Netflix is going to launch a TikTok-like vertical video feed within its apps this month, and plans to use AI broadly for content creation and recommendations.

items.title: SaySo is a new short-form video app that aims to restore users’ trust in news
items.description: Users are fed up with misinformation and AI slop cluttering their feeds. SaySo is a new short-form video app that delivers news from vetted creators and journalists.

items.title: Loop raises $95M to build supply chain AI that predicts disruptions
items.description: The San Francisco startup closed a Series C funding round led by Antonio Gracias' firm Valor, which is a major backer of xAI.
